title: Fractional CMO vs. Full-Time Marketing Director | Daniel Fox
meta_description: The full-time marketing director a $3 to $12M company can afford usually buys hands, not judgment. How fractional leadership compares, and when each one fits. Daniel Fox.

mainEntity.name: Fractional CMO vs. full-time marketing director: which is better?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a $3 to $12M company, the full-time marketing director you can actually afford usually buys execution, not strategic judgment. A real CMO who can author the direction runs about $300,000 to $450,000 a year all in, which is not what a company at this stage is hiring at. So the affordable full-time seat tends to fail in one of two ways: a capable executor with no one authoring strategy, or a real executive underused on overhead. A fractional CMO puts senior judgment on top of the hands you already have, part-time, without funding the overhead of the seat. Daniel Fox is a fractional B2C CMO who takes that strategic seat.
